page: Amazon Web Services r8g.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom 64-bit ARM architecture. The system features 192 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor model running at 2.7 GHz, with one thread per core. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or discrete GPU hardware, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core and memory performance. The multi-core stress-ng CPU test and Geekbench multi-core scores both rank in the top 10% of tested systems, alongside top-tier memory bandwidth results, such as 1084.93 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations. Database operations and Redis SET operations also achieve top-tier status, with Redis reaching 363.6 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. Additionally, LLM inference prompt processing for small 135M models is relatively weak, landing in the bottom 25%, while prompt processing for medium 7B models registers as strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8g.48xlarge offers high memory density and massive parallel compute capabilities on energy-efficient ARM architecture, making it highly resource-dense for large-scale deployments. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, focusing the hardware profile entirely on memory-bound and CPU-intensive execution. This server is suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, distributed caching systems like Redis, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and 192 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARM AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.7 GHz. It features 1536.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core, and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results place its multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations in the top 10% tier, while single-core metrics remain in the average range. This resource-dense profile is highly efficient for parallel processing but presents a tradeoff in single-threaded tasks. The instance is suited for large-scale database operations, Redis caching, software compilation, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.48xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server featuring 192 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 1536 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.48xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 192 physical cores, 1536 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized ARM64 platform delivering exceptional multi-core throughput and massive memory bandwidth for enterprise database and caching workloads.

Equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
